rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 10.3.1 Hotfix 1014002.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 10.3.1 Hotfix 1014002. Product: Forms 10.3.1 KB: 1014002 Published: August 24, 2018 Last Updated: August 24, 2018 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche Forms 10.3.1 Hotfix 1014002. The hotfix resolves the following issues involving uploading processes: You no longer receive an "An unexpected error has occurred. [LFF502-UnexpectedError]" error message when uploading a process definition that includes a deleted sub-process. (90299) You no longer receive an "An unexpected error has occurred. [LFF502-UnexpectedError]" error message when uploading a process definition and choosing to overwrite an existing process and the process includes prior completed submissions that include a now deleted task. (101078) The hotfix includes the following updated file: FormsModel.dll (10.3.1.647) Resolution Click the following link to download a zip file containing the updated file. KB1014002.zip Create a backup of the existing FormsModel.dll in the bin subfolder of the Laserfiche Forms installation folder. By default, the path is C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Laserfiche Forms\Forms\bin. Extract and place the updated FormsModel.dll in the folder. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

(155500) The Retrieve Field Values activity no longer errors out with a "System.InvalidCastException" error when processing a Data type field that contains data. (1551... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013837/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-wo ...
rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche Workflow 10.2 Hotfix 1013837.
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche Workflow 10.2 Hotfix 1013837.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche Workflow 10.2 Hotfix 1013837. Product: Workflow 10.2 KB: 1013837 Published: April 4, 2017 Last Updated: September 11, 2023 Summary This article details the changes for the Laserfiche Workflow 10.2 Hotfix 1013837. When using the Spanish version of Workflow, certain security settings may prevent the Web Client from displaying business processes. This issue is now resolved after you republish any affected business processes. (155950) The For Each Entry activity now uses the same date format as the Workflow server. (155528) When Workflow uses the column token from a Forms table to populate the fields in a multi-value field group, the blank value at the top of a column token is now preserved. (155527) Workflow may be unable to start workflows when the System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing security setting is enabled. After installing the hotfix, add a FIPSOverride DWORD value to the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Laserfiche\Workflow key and set the value to 1. Restart the Workflow Server service after creating the key. (155500) The Retrieve Field Values activity no longer errors out with a "System.InvalidCastException" error when processing a Data type field that contains data. (155167) When you create a workflow that uses an Assign Field Values activity, and assign a value from a token to a list field, you can validate the workflow without receiving a warning that field definitions were changed. (154781) You no longer receive an "Invalid operation: 'XmlUtility:GetAttributeAsBoolean:0' [0009-B1]" error message when using the Retrieve Business Process Variables activity to select Form fields from a Forms 10.1 Server. (154044) The Email activity now properly supports the (Beta) Optimize compression for text on color images option. Previously, enabling the option could cause an "Entry point was not found" error. (153939) The Set Business Process Variables activity now properly supports setting the time in a Date field in Forms 10.2. (153780) The update includes the following files: Laserfiche.Workflow.Lfso.dll (assembly version 8.3.0.0, file version 10.2.0.216) Laserfiche.Workflow.Activities.83.dll (file version 10.2.0.216) Laserfiche.Workflow.Runtime.dll (assembly version 8.3.0.0, file version 10.2.0.216) Laserfiche.Workflow.Activities.83.Processor,exe (file version 10.2.0.216) Laserfiche.Workflow.Activities.83.External.dll (file version 10.2.0.216) Laserfiche.Workflow.Service.exe (file version 10.0.0.216) Laserfiche.Workflow.Subscriber.exe (file version 10.0.0.216) Laserfiche.Workflow.Designer.exe (file version 10.0.0.216) Laserfiche.Workflow.Compiler.exe (file version 10.0.0.216) Laserfiche.Workflow.BusinessProcess.dll (assembly version 10.0.0.0, file version 10.0.0.333) Laserfiche.Workflow.RA.dll (assembly version 8.3.0.0, file version 10.2.0.216) Resolution To update Workflow 10.2 Click one of the following links to download either the 64-bit version (if you are on a x64 machine) or the 32-bit version (if you are on a x86 machine) of the update. KB1013837_x64.zip for the x64 machine KB1013837_x86.zip for the x86 machine Each zip file contains an executable file (.exe). Extract the contents of the zip file to a temporary location. If necessary, transfer the appropriate .exe file(s) to the correct computers and double-click the appropriate .exe to update the desired Workflow component. Note: The update file automatically checks for whether the Workflow Server, Subscriber, or Designer is installed and updates the appropriate files as needed. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: An "Operation timed out. [784]" Error Occurs When Logging In To Laserfiche 8 Or Higher Using Windows Authentication. 12 years ago An "Operation timed out. [784]" Error Occurs When Logging In To Laserfiche 8 Or Higher Using Windows Authentication. February 4, 2014 | KB: 1012887 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012887/an-operation-timed-out-784-err ...
rows.title: An "Operation timed out. [784]" Error Occurs When Logging In To Laserfiche 8 Or Higher Using Windows Authentication.
rows.detailTitle: An "Operation timed out. [784]" Error Occurs When Logging In To Laserfiche 8 Or Higher Using Windows Authentication.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in An "Operation timed out. [784]" Error Occurs When Logging In To Laserfiche 8 Or Higher Using Windows Authentication. Product: Laserfiche 8, Laserfiche 9 KB: 1012887 Published: December 19, 2011 Last Updated: February 4, 2014 Summary When logging in to your Laserfiche 8 or higher Repository through Windows Authentication, you may receive the following error message: Operation timed out. [784] When you try to log in using a Laserfiche user account, the process completes successfully. Cause In certain network configurations, there may be communications delays. One possible scenario is if you are connected to the network over VPN from a distant location. A delay in retrieving or verifying Windows user credentials during the login process can cause the Laserfiche Client to time out while attempting to log in to the Laserfiche Server. Workaround You can extend the client-side login timeout for LFSO client applications by adding a registry value. Under the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Laserfiche\LFSO\version key, create a DWORD value named LoginTimeoutSeconds. Set the value of LoginTimeoutSeconds to the number of seconds to wait before timing out. The default value is 20 seconds. Replace version with the appropriate version number for your client program in major.minor format. For example, with Laserfiche Client 9.0.x, use the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Laserfiche\LFSO\9.0 key. With Laserfiche Client 8.3.x, look for the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Laserfiche\LFSO\8.3 key. Note: If you are on a 64-bit version of Windows, use the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Wow6432node\Laserfiche\LFSO\version key. Note: The LoginTimeoutSeconds registry value is available for Laserfiche Client 8.1 and later. Note: Manually create the LFSO or versionnumber keys if necessary. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: An "Error 797" Occurs When Connecting to Laserfiche Server. 12 years ago attempting to connect to a Laserfiche Server 8, the following error occurs: Error 797 This error may occur when connecting from a Laserfiche client or a custom client. Resolutions... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012568/an-error-797-occurs-when-c ...
rows.title: An "Error 797" Occurs When Connecting to Laserfiche Server.
rows.detailTitle: An "Error 797" Occurs When Connecting to Laserfiche Server.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Error 797" Occurs When Connecting to Laserfiche Server. Product: Laserfiche 8, Laserfiche 9 KB: 1012568 Published: April 5, 2010 Last Updated: October 28, 2013 Summary When attempting to connect to a Laserfiche Server 8, the following error occurs: Error 797 This error may occur when connecting from a Laserfiche client or a custom client. Resolutions Depending on your situation, try one or more of the following: Ensure you are providing the correct server name or IP address to the client. If you are using a Laserfiche Client, reattach the repository. In addition, if you are on a Windows Vista, Windows Server 2008, or Windows 7 machine, disable User Account Control (UAC), reattach, then enable UAC. If this does not resolve the issue, and you are not using a web-based Laserfiche client, repair the client installation. If you are running antivirus software, ensure LF.exe is exempted from network scanner checks and that HTTP traffic on the Laserfiche Server listening port (80 or 5050, by default) is not blocked. Alternatively, disable the anti-virus software, if that is possible. If there is a firewall (such as the Windows firewall) between the Laserfiche Server and the client, ensure the Laserfiche Server listening port (80 or 5050, by default) is open. For more information, search the Laserfiche Administrator's Guide for "Firewalls." Ensure you are not attempting to connect to a Laserfiche 7 Server using a Laserfiche 8 Client. The Server version number should always match or be greater than the Client version number. More Information Please see the following knowledge base articles for more information: ERROR: A "Could not connect to the server. (797)" Error Occurs When Connecting to Laserfiche With Windows Authentication. LFSO Tracing is one way to log communication between Laserfiche Server and client workstations. Support professionals can use the log to resolve the problem. If none of the steps above resolves the issue, see the following article to configure LFSO Tracing on the client machine: HOW TO: Configuring LFSO Tracing for Laserfiche Workstations Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "No response receive from the server. [780]" Error Occurs When Merging Field Definitions in the Laserfiche 8 Administration Console. 15 years ago A "No response receive from the server. [780]" Error Occurs When Merging Field Definitions in the Laserfiche 8 Administration Console. November 15, 2010 | KB: 1012708 https://support.... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012708/a-no-response-receive-from-the-se ...
rows.title: A "No response receive from the server. [780]" Error Occurs When Merging Field Definitions in the Laserfiche 8 Administration Console.
rows.detailTitle: A "No response receive from the server. [780]" Error Occurs When Merging Field Definitions in the Laserfiche 8 Administration Console.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in A "No response receive from the server. [780]" Error Occurs When Merging Field Definitions in the Laserfiche 8 Administration Console. Product: Laserfiche 8, Laserfiche 9 KB: 1012708 Published: November 15, 2010 Last Updated: November 15, 2010 Summary When merging field definitions that are associated with a large number of field values in the Laserfiche 8 Administration Console, you may receive the following error message after 15 minutes: No Response received from the server. [780] Cause There is a default 15 minute timeout when waiting for the first byte of the Laserfiche Server's response to a request. When merging field definitions that involve a large number of field values, the process may exceed the timeout. Workaround Edit the registry to change the timeout value. Modify the ReceiveTimeoutSeconds DWORD value under the "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Laserfiche \LFSO\8.1" registry key. On a 64-bit version of Windows, look under the "KEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Laserfiche \LFSO\8.1" registry key. Note: This workaround only applies for Laserfiche 8.1.x. To modify the ReceiveTimeoutSeconds registry value Click Start and click Run. (On Windows 7 or Windows Server 2008, type the following step into the search box in the Start menu). Type the following and then click OK to load the Windows Registry Editor. regedit Expand HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE. Expand SOFTWARE. (On a 64-bit version of Windows, expand Wow6432Node). Expand Laserfiche. Expand LFSO. Expand 8.1. Create a new DWORD value named: ReceiveTimeoutSeconds Set the value to 0 to remove the timeout. Note: You may need to create the LFSO and 8.1 subkeys. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: List of Changes for Directory Server 10.3.
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Directory Server 10.3.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Directory Server 10.3. Product: Directory Server 10.3 KB: 1013928 Published: February 13, 2018 Last Updated: July 27, 2020 Summary Laserfiche Directory Server 10.3 features initial support for SAML identity providers. In addition, Directory Server now logs licensing changes and the administration site includes a reporting feature to report on those changes. SAML Identity Providers Directory Server now supports connecting to SAML 2.0 identity providers. This initial implementation supports allows importing SAML metadata and configuring claim mappings for single sign-on support. Testing was done primarily with a Shibboleth identity provider solution. Audit Reporting Directory Server now creates an audit log for licensing site changes. The Directory Server administration site now includes an Audit Reports page where you can run reports on the available audit data. Choose from multiple event types like user creation and license assignment while also reporting on a specific date range. Additional Changes and Fixes The process user license type is now supported in the Directory Server administration site. Directory Server is now more fault-tolerant when processing certain types of invalid Rio primary licenses in order to more easily facilitate troubleshooting and reactivation. There are now more options for customizing the look of the AD FS sign-in button. The Settings page now allows you to configure the SQL connection string to your licensing site SQL Server database. (36111) Active Directory Group Synchronization rules can now successfully process Active Group groups with large memberships. (62084) The user profile tab no longer displays a "Cannot get user information" error for large profiles (e.g., if the profile contains a profile image). (36321) Directory Server has improved handling of the participant license type. (36311, 36332, 36339, 36337, 36347) More Information 1013927 Release Notes for Laserfiche Directory Server 10.3. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche Directory Server 10.3.1.
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche Directory Server 10.3.1.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Laserfiche Directory Server 10.3.1. Product: Directory Server 10.3.1 KB: 1013977 Published: September 18, 2018 Last Updated: July 27, 2020 Summary Laserfiche Directory Server 10.3.1 adds discovery service support for SAML identity providers as well as stability and performance improvements. See the following list of new features: Directory Server now includes initial SAML discovery service support. When configuring the STS Site, administrators now have an option to specify a SAML identity provider discovery endpoint value. On the SAML Identity Provider property page, there is also now an option to hide the identity provider button on the Directory Server sign-in page. The Directory Server web administration console now includes a "Sign in as..." option available from the upper-right user drop-down menu. Use the option to sign out and sign in. There is now an option to automatically renew a primary license prior to the primary license expiration date. Turn on the renew subscription primary license automatically option on the Settings tab to take advantage of the automatic renewal. Directory Server will start attempting to renew an expiring primary license 30 days before the expiration date. The Users page now defaults to listing the first 1500 users when you first view the page. Use the new search options on the page to view additional users. Directory Server now turns on the Trim Audit Reporting Data option by default. Additional Changes and Fixes Signing in through Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS) now properly inherits Windows group memberships. (101053) You can specify a value higher than 24 hours for the Poll Active Directory for synchronization every x hours option. (84829) You can now properly assign licenses allocated to organizations to devices. (76584) In certain configurations you could see ArgumentOutOfRangeException errors involving LicenseManagerWebSTS.Utility.Localization.RetrieveSupportedLanguages in the Windows Event log. This issue is now resolved. (36265) More Information 1013976 Release Notes for Laserfiche Directory Server 10.3.1. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: List of Changes for Directory Server 10.4.3
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Directory Server 10.4.3
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Directory Server 10.4.3 Product: Directory Server 10.4.3 KB: 1014096 Published: December 18, 2019 Last Updated: July 27, 2020 Summary Laserfiche Directory Server 10.4.3 includes the following new features: Two-Step Verification for Laserfiche Users Directory Server now supports two-step verification for Laserfiche Users. Directory Server administrators can require MFA for Laserfiche Users. Upon next sign-in, users will be presented with instructions to link their account with a one-time passcode authenticator app (e.g., Google Authenticator or Microsoft Authenticator). Scheduling Active Directory Synchronization Directory Server administrators can now configure scheduled windows for Active Directory synchronization. Specify a time of day as well as days of the week to run synchronization rules. Additional Changes Directory Server 10.4.3 no longer relies on WCF bindings for communication with STS instances. The XmlEndpointUtility.exe and STSEndpointUtility.exe utilities now include a new section for configuring the HTTPS binding between Directory Server and STS instances. Directory Server administrators can now choose what Directory Server audit events types to log. Directory Server now allows Active Directory domain user to be authenticated through a SAML identity provider. The user listing page now always displays filtering options. Directory Server now supports encrypted SAML tokens. Provide decryption certificate information when configuring the SAML identity provider. The STS configuration page now includes options to hide the Laserfiche User authentication elements and the Windows Authentication button on the Directory Server sign-in page. Fixes You no longer receive a "The directory property cannot be found in the cache." error message when renewing a primary license and Directory Server is installed in a failover cluster. (156272) You no longer receive a "String was not recognized as a valid DateTime." error message when Directory Server attempts to trim audit reporting data in certain scenarios involving certain time formats. (161718) In certain scenarios where you are licensed for the Rio Public Portal for Unlimited Laserfiche Servers portal add-on, the Register Application Instance page now displays Multiprocessor License instead of None in the How would public users access WebLink section. (170280) In certain scenarios, the Create User page could incorrectly switch the displayed values of remaining licenses. This issue is now resolved. (200140) Related Links 1014097 Release Notes for Laserfiche Directory Server 10.4.3. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark
